subsystems.name: reactor_power
subsystems.note: Kilopower / fission surface power has NO flight heritage. SNAP-10A (1965) partial-failed after 43 days. 0.95 is a placeholder lift from RTG heritage (which is excellent: Voyager / Cassini / NH all >0.99 over 10-30 yr); active fission is meaningfully harder.

subsystems.name: primary_propulsion
subsystems.note: Deep-space ion-thruster heritage from Dawn (3 thrusters, 1 grid wear failure, system survived) and Hayabusa (4 thrusters, multiple anomalies, mission limped). 0.90 per system. Water-MET is HERITAGE-NONE and likely lower without redundancy.

subsystems.name: rcs
subsystems.note: Standard monoprop or cold-gas RCS heritage is high. Cassini, Juno, etc.

subsystems.name: gnc_compute
subsystems.note: Flight-computer + IMU + star-tracker chain. Reaction-wheel anomalies are common (Kepler, Hayabusa, MRO, Dawn) and bring the chain reliability below the individual-component spec.

subsystems.name: comms
subsystems.note: HGA + radio + DSN. Galileo HGA failure is the canonical degradation (brought Galileo mass-return to ~0.70 not 1.0). Voyager 49 yr operating is the upper-bound demonstration.

subsystems.name: bag_harvest
subsystems.note: No flight heritage for ring-mining bag at this scale. AstroMesh-class deployable structures have >0.98 heritage but at radio-frequency mesh apertures, not 1 m apertures. Bag-engineering doc treats this as the highest-risk subsystem; 0.85 is the analyst's paper estimate.

subsystems.name: thermal_control
subsystems.note: Cruise spacecraft thermal heritage is high (~0.99 per loop). Megawatt-class radiator stack with two-phase ammonia loop is heritage-adjacent (ISS) but never run in cruise for 14 yr. 0.95 is the paper estimate.

subsystems.name: return_handoff
subsystems.note: Sample-return / customer-interface heritage from Stardust, OSIRIS-REx, Hayabusa2. 0.95 is the heritage average; ICEBERG hand-off is propellant transfer, which is technically harder than passive sample release.
